---
title: "Olympic Games"
format: 
  dashboard:
    orientation: columns
embed-resources: true
logo: olympics-logo.svg
logo-alt: "Olympics logo with multicolored circles."
---

```{r}
#| label: load-packages
library(tidyverse)
library(gt)
```

```{r}
#| label: load-data
olympics_full <- read_csv("olympics.csv")
```

```{r}
#| label: prep-data
olympics <- olympics_full |>
  filter(
    season == "Summer",
    !is.na(medal)
  ) |>
  separate_wider_delim(
    cols = team, 
    names = c("team", "suffix"), 
    delim = "-", 
    too_many = "merge", 
    too_few = "align_start"
  ) |>
  select(-suffix) |>
  mutate(medal = fct_relevel(medal, "Gold", "Silver", "Bronze"))
```

## Medals by sport and year

### Medals by sport

```{r}
#| label: medals-by-sport

olympics |>
  mutate(
    sport = fct_lump_n(sport, n = 15),
    sport = fct_infreq(sport),
    sport = fct_rev(sport)
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = sport, fill = medal)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Medal"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +  
  guides(fill = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +             
  theme(
    legend.position = "inside", 
    legend.position.inside = c(.75, .15), 
    legend.direction = "horizontal", 
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", 
                                     color = "black")
  )
```

### Medals by year

:::{.card title="Medals by year"}

Due to WWII, no Olympic games were held in 1940 and 1944.
```{r}
#| label: medals-by-year
#| title: Medals by year

olympics |>
  count(year, medal) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = n, color = medal)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = NULL,
    color = "Medal"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +                     
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "inside",
    legend.position.inside = c(0.25, 0.75), 
    legend.direction = "horizontal", 
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", 
                                     color = "black") 
  )
```

:::

## Row {width="45%"}

### Column <!-- for some reason no matter what values I tried (25-100, even 200) it was a really small squished cube... -->

```{r}
most_gold <- olympics |>
  filter(medal == "Gold") |>
  count(team, sort = TRUE) |>
  slice_head(n = 1)

most_silver <- olympics |>
  filter(medal == "Silver") |>
  count(team, sort = TRUE) |>
  slice_head(n = 1)

most_bronze <- olympics |>
  filter(medal == "Bronze") |>
  count(team, sort = TRUE) |>
  slice_head(n = 1)
```

::: {.valuebox icon="award-fill" color="#D4AF37"}
Most Golds:

`{r} most_gold$n`

`{r} most_gold$team`

:::

::: {.valuebox icon="award-fill" color="#C0C0C0"}
Most Silver:

`{r} most_silver$n`

`{r} most_silver$team`

:::

::: {.valuebox icon="award-fill" color="#CD7F32"}
Most Bronze:

`{r} most_bronze$n`

`{r} most_bronze$team`

:::

### Medals by country

::: panel-tabset
## Top 30 total medals

</br>

Teams sorted in descending order of total medals.

```{r}
#| label: top-30

olympics |>
  count(team, medal) |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = medal,
    values_from = n,
    values_fill = 0
  ) |>
  slice_max(n = 30, order_by = Gold) |>
  select(team, Gold, Silver, Bronze) |>
  gt() |>
  data_color(
    columns = Gold,
    colors = scales::col_numeric(c("#FFF7CC", "#D4AF37"), domain = NULL)
  ) |>
  data_color(
    columns = Silver,
    colors = scales::col_numeric(c("#F2F2F2", "#C0C0C0"), domain = NULL)
  ) |>
  data_color(
    columns = Bronze,
    colors = scales::col_numeric(c("#F5E1D3", "#CD7F32"), domain = NULL)
  )
```

## Bottom 30 total medals

</br>

Teams sorted in descending order of total medals.

```{r}
#| label: bottom-30

olympics |>
  count(team, medal) |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = medal,
    values_from = n,
    values_fill = 0
  ) |>
  slice_min(n = 30, order_by = Gold) |>
  select(team, Gold, Silver, Bronze) |>
  gt() |>
  data_color(
    columns = Gold,
    colors = scales::col_numeric(c("#FFF7CC", "#D4AF37"), domain = NULL)
  ) |>
  data_color(
    columns = Silver,
    colors = scales::col_numeric(c("#F2F2F2", "#C0C0C0"), domain = NULL)
  ) |>
  data_color(
    columns = Bronze,
    colors = scales::col_numeric(c("#F5E1D3", "#CD7F32"), domain = NULL)
  )
```
:::
